params.name: organization-info-address-address-lines
params.description: Unstructured address lines describing the lower levels of an address. Because values in `address_lines` do not have type information and may sometimes contain multiple values in a single field (for example, 'Austin, TX'), it is important that the line order is clear. The order of address lines should be 'envelope order' for the country or region of the address. In places where this can vary (for example, Japan), `address_language` is used to make it explicit (for example, 'ja' for large-to-small ordering and 'ja-Latn' or 'en' for small-to-large). In this way, the most specific line of an address can be selected based on the language. The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a `region_code` with all remaining information placed in the `address_lines`. It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved. Creating an address only containing a `region_code` and `address_lines` and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

params.name: organization-info-address-administrative-area
params.description: Optional. Highest administrative subdivision which is used for postal addresses of a country or region. For example, this can be a state, a province, an oblast, or a prefecture. For Spain, this is the province and not the autonomous community (for example, 'Barcelona' and not 'Catalonia'). Many countries don't use an administrative area in postal addresses. For example, in Switzerland, this should be left unpopulated.

params.name: organization-info-address-language-code
params.description: Optional. BCP-47 language code of the contents of this address (if known). This is often the UI language of the input form or is expected to match one of the languages used in the address' country/region, or their transliterated equivalents. This can affect formatting in certain countries, but is not critical to the correctness of the data and will never affect any validation or other non-formatting related operations. If this value is not known, it should be omitted (rather than specifying a possibly incorrect default). Examples: 'zh-Hant', 'ja', 'ja-Latn', 'en'.

params.name: organization-info-address-locality
params.description: Optional. Generally refers to the city or town portion of the address. Examples: US city, IT comune, UK post town. In regions of the world where localities are not well defined or do not fit into this structure well, leave `locality` empty and use `address_lines`.

params.name: organization-info-address-organization
params.description: Optional. The name of the organization at the address.

params.name: organization-info-address-postal-code
params.description: Optional. Postal code of the address. Not all countries use or require postal codes to be present, but where they are used, they may trigger additional validation with other parts of the address (for example, state or zip code validation in the United States).

params.name: organization-info-address-recipients
params.description: Optional. The recipient at the address. This field may, under certain circumstances, contain multiline information. For example, it might contain 'care of' information.

params.name: organization-info-address-region-code
params.description: Required. CLDR region code of the country/region of the address. This is never inferred and it is up to the user to ensure the value is correct. See https://cldr.unicode.org/ and https://www.unicode.org/cldr/charts/30/supplemental/territory_information.html for details. Example: 'CH' for Switzerland.

params.name: organization-info-address-revision
params.description: The schema revision of the `PostalAddress`. This must be set to 0, which is the latest revision. All new revisions **must** be backward compatible with old revisions.

params.name: organization-info-address-sorting-code
params.description: Optional. Additional, country-specific, sorting code. This is not used in most regions. Where it is used, the value is either a string like 'CEDEX', optionally followed by a number (for example, 'CEDEX 7'), or just a number alone, representing the 'sector code' (Jamaica), 'delivery area indicator' (Malawi) or 'post office indicator' (Côte d'Ivoire).

params.name: organization-info-address-sublocality
params.description: Optional. Sublocality of the address. For example, this can be a neighborhood, borough, or district.

params.name: organization-info-phone-number
params.description: Output only. The contact number for the organization.

params.name: organization-info-registered-domain

The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a `region_code` with all remaining information placed in the `address_lines`. It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved. Creating an address only containing a `region_code` and `address_lines` and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

params.name: storefront-address-address-lines
params.description: Unstructured address lines describing the lower levels of an address. Because values in `address_lines` do not have type information and may sometimes contain multiple values in a single field (for example, 'Austin, TX'), it is important that the line order is clear. The order of address lines should be 'envelope order' for the country or region of the address. In places where this can vary (for example, Japan), `address_language` is used to make it explicit (for example, 'ja' for large-to-small ordering and 'ja-Latn' or 'en' for small-to-large). In this way, the most specific line of an address can be selected based on the language. The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a `region_code` with all remaining information placed in the `address_lines`. It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved. Creating an address only containing a `region_code` and `address_lines` and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

params.name: storefront-address-administrative-area
params.description: Optional. Highest administrative subdivision which is used for postal addresses of a country or region. For example, this can be a state, a province, an oblast, or a prefecture. For Spain, this is the province and not the autonomous community (for example, 'Barcelona' and not 'Catalonia'). Many countries don't use an administrative area in postal addresses. For example, in Switzerland, this should be left unpopulated.

params.name: storefront-address-language-code
params.description: Optional. BCP-47 language code of the contents of this address (if known). This is often the UI language of the input form or is expected to match one of the languages used in the address' country/region, or their transliterated equivalents. This can affect formatting in certain countries, but is not critical to the correctness of the data and will never affect any validation or other non-formatting related operations. If this value is not known, it should be omitted (rather than specifying a possibly incorrect default). Examples: 'zh-Hant', 'ja', 'ja-Latn', 'en'.

params.name: storefront-address-locality
params.description: Optional. Generally refers to the city or town portion of the address. Examples: US city, IT comune, UK post town. In regions of the world where localities are not well defined or do not fit into this structure well, leave `locality` empty and use `address_lines`.

params.name: storefront-address-organization
params.description: Optional. The name of the organization at the address.

params.name: storefront-address-postal-code
params.description: Optional. Postal code of the address. Not all countries use or require postal codes to be present, but where they are used, they may trigger additional validation with other parts of the address (for example, state or zip code validation in the United States).

params.name: storefront-address-recipients
params.description: Optional. The recipient at the address. This field may, under certain circumstances, contain multiline information. For example, it might contain 'care of' information.

params.name: storefront-address-region-code
params.description: Required. CLDR region code of the country/region of the address. This is never inferred and it is up to the user to ensure the value is correct. See https://cldr.unicode.org/ and https://www.unicode.org/cldr/charts/30/supplemental/territory_information.html for details. Example: 'CH' for Switzerland.

params.name: storefront-address-revision
params.description: The schema revision of the `PostalAddress`. This must be set to 0, which is the latest revision. All new revisions **must** be backward compatible with old revisions.

params.name: storefront-address-sorting-code
params.description: Optional. Additional, country-specific, sorting code. This is not used in most regions. Where it is used, the value is either a string like 'CEDEX', optionally followed by a number (for example, 'CEDEX 7'), or just a number alone, representing the 'sector code' (Jamaica), 'delivery area indicator' (Malawi) or 'post office indicator' (Côte d'Ivoire).

params.name: storefront-address-sublocality
params.description: Optional. Sublocality of the address. For example, this can be a neighborhood, borough, or district.

params.name: title
params.description: Required. Location name should reflect your business's real-world name, as used consistently on your storefront, website, and stationery, and as known to customers. Any additional information, when relevant, can be included in other fields of the resource (for example, `Address`, `Categories`). Don't add unnecessary information to your name (for example, prefer 'Google' over 'Google Inc. - Mountain View Corporate Headquarters'). Don't include marketing taglines, store codes, special characters, hours or closed/open status, phone numbers, website URLs, service/product information, location/address or directions, or containment information (for example, 'Chase ATM in Duane Reade').

params.name: websiteUri
params.description: Optional. A URL for this business. If possible, use a URL that represents this individual business location instead of a generic website/URL that represents all locations, or the brand.

params.name: accessibility-mobility-accessible-elevator-exception
params.description: Mobility accessible elevator exception.

params.name: accessibility-mobility-accessible-exception
params.description: Mobility accessible exception.

params.description: Mobility accessible pool exception.

params.name: activities-beach-front-exception
params.description: Beach front exception.

params.name: activities-bicycle-rental

params.name: activities-bicycle-rental-exception

params.name: activities-boutique-stores-exception

params.name: activities-casino

params.name: activities-free-bicycle-rental

params.name: activities-free-bicycle-rental-exception

params.name: activities-free-watercraft-rental

params.name: activities-free-watercraft-rental-exception
params.description: Free Watercraft rental exception.

May also include non-electronic games like pool, foosball, darts, and more. May or may not be designed for children. Also known as arcade, fun room, or family fun center.

params.name: activities-game-room-exception
params.description: Game room exception.

params.name: activities-golf

params.name: activities-golf-exception
params.description: Golf exception.

params.name: activities-horseback-riding
params.description: Horseback riding. The hotel has a horse barn onsite or an affiliation with a nearby barn to allow for guests to sit astride a horse and direct it to walk, trot, cantor, gallop and/or jump. Can be in a riding ring, on designated paths, or in the wilderness. May or may not involve instruction.

params.name: activities-horseback-riding-exception
params.description: Horseback riding exception.

params.name: activities-nightclub
params.description: Nightclub. There is a room at the hotel with a bar, a dance floor, and seating where designated staffers play dance music. There may also be a designated area for the performance of live music, singing and comedy acts.

params.name: activities-nightclub-exception
params.description: Nightclub exception.

params.name: activities-private-beach
params.description: Private beach. The beach which is in close proximity to the hotel is open only to guests.

params.name: activities-private-beach-exception
params.description: Private beach exception.

params.name: activities-scuba
params.description: Scuba. The provision for guests to dive under naturally occurring water fitted with a self-contained underwater breathing apparatus (SCUBA) for the purpose of exploring underwater life. Apparatus consists of a tank providing oxygen to the diver through a mask. Requires certification of the diver and supervision. The hotel may have the activity at its own waterfront or have an affiliation with a nearby facility. Required equipment is most often supplied to guests. Can be free or for a fee. Not snorkeling. Not done in a swimming pool.

params.name: activities-scuba-exception
params.description: Scuba exception.

params.name: activities-snorkeling
params.description: Snorkeling. The provision for guests to participate in a recreational water activity in which swimmers wear a diving mask, a simple, shaped breathing tube and flippers/swim fins for the purpose of exploring below the surface of an ocean, gulf or lake. Does not usually require user certification or professional supervision. Equipment may or may not be available for rent or purchase. Not scuba diving.

params.name: activities-snorkeling-exception
params.description: Snorkeling exception.

params.name: activities-tennis
params.description: Tennis. The hotel has the requisite court(s) on site or has an affiliation with a nearby facility for the purpose of providing guests with the opportunity to play a two-sided court-based game in which players use a stringed racquet to hit a ball across a net to the side of the opposing player. The court can be indoors or outdoors. Instructors, racquets and balls may or may not be provided.

params.name: activities-tennis-exception
params.description: Tennis exception.

params.name: activities-water-skiing

params.name: activities-water-skiing-exception

params.name: activities-watercraft-rental

params.name: activities-watercraft-rental-exception

params.name: policies-all-inclusive-only-exception

params.name: policies-checkin-time-hours
params.description: Hours of a day in 24 hour format. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 23. An API may choose to allow the value '24:00:00' for scenarios like business closing time.

params.name: policies-checkin-time-minutes
params.description: Minutes of an hour. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 59.

params.name: policies-checkin-time-nanos
params.description: Fractions of seconds, in nanoseconds. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 999,999,999.

params.name: policies-checkin-time-seconds
params.description: Seconds of a minute. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 59. An API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

params.name: policies-checkin-time-exception
params.description: Check-in time exception.

params.name: policies-checkout-time-hours
params.description: Hours of a day in 24 hour format. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must be less than or equal to 23. An API may choose to allow the value '24:00:00' for scenarios like business closing time.

params.name: policies-checkout-time-minutes
params.description: Minutes of an hour. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 59.

params.name: policies-checkout-time-nanos

params.name: policies-checkout-time-seconds

params.name: policies-checkout-time-exception

params.name: policies-max-kids-stay-free-count-exception

params.name: policies-payment-options-mobile-nfc-exception

params.name: policies-smoke-free-property

params.name: policies-smoke-free-property-exception

params.name: pools-adult-pool-exception

Can be indoors or outdoors. Not used for active swimming. Also known as Jacuzzi. Hot tub must be in a common area where all guests can access it. Does not apply to room-specific hot tubs that are only accessible to guest occupying that room.

params.name: pools-hot-tub-exception

params.name: pools-indoor-pool
params.description: Indoor pool. A pool located inside the hotel and available for guests to use for swimming and/or soaking. Use may or may not be restricted to adults and/or children.

params.name: pools-lazy-river

params.name: pools-lazy-river-exception

params.name: pools-lifeguard

params.name: pools-lifeguard-exception

params.name: pools-outdoor-pool

params.name: pools-outdoor-pool-exception

params.description: Pool. The presence of a pool, either indoors or outdoors, for guests to use for swimming and/or soaking. Use may or may not be restricted to adults and/or children.

params.name: pools-pool-exception
params.description: Pool exception.

params.name: pools-pools-count

params.name: pools-water-park

params.name: pools-water-park-exception

params.name: pools-waterslide

params.name: pools-waterslide-exception
params.description: Waterslide exception.

params.name: pools-wave-pool
params.description: Wave pool. A large indoor or outdoor pool with a machine that produces water currents to mimic the ocean's crests.

params.name: pools-wave-pool-exception
params.description: Wave pool exception.

params.name: property-built-year

params.name: property-built-year-exception

params.name: property-floors-count
params.description: Floors count. The number of stories the building has from the ground floor to the top floor that are accessible to guests.

params.name: property-floors-count-exception
params.description: Floors count exception.

params.name: property-last-renovated-year

params.name: property-rooms-count

params.name: property-rooms-count-exception
params.description: Rooms count exception.

params.name: services-baggage-storage-exception
params.description: Baggage storage exception.

params.name: services-concierge
params.description: Concierge. Hotel staff member(s) responsible for facilitating an easy, comfortable stay through making reservations for meals, sourcing theater tickets, arranging tours, finding a doctor, making recommendations, and answering questions.

params.name: services-concierge-exception
params.description: Concierge exception.

params.name: services-convenience-store-exception
params.description: Convenience store exception.

params.name: services-currency-exchange-exception

params.name: services-elevator-exception
params.description: Elevator exception.

params.name: services-front-desk
params.description: Front desk. A counter or desk in the lobby or the immediate interior of the hotel where a member of the staff greets guests and processes the information related to their stay (including check-in and check-out). May or may not be manned and open 24/7.

params.name: services-front-desk-exception
params.description: Front desk exception.

params.name: services-full-service-laundry

params.name: services-gift-shop

params.name: services-languages-spoken

params.description: Executive floor. A floor of the hotel where the guestrooms are only bookable by members of the hotel's frequent guest membership program. Benefits of this room class include access to a designated lounge which may or may not feature free breakfast, cocktails or other perks specific to members of the program.

params.name: some-units-executive-floor-exception

params.description: Max occupants count exception.

params.name: some-units-private-home-exception

params.description: Lake view.

params.description: Valley view exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-carbon-free-energy-sources
params.description: Carbon free energy sources. Property sources carbon-free electricity via at least one of the following methods: on-site clean energy generation, power purchase agreement(s) with clean energy generators, green power provided by electricity supplier, or purchases of Energy Attribute Certificates (such as Renewable Energy Certificates or Guarantees of Origin).

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-carbon-free-energy-sources-exception
params.description: Carbon free energy sources exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-conservation-program
params.description: Energy conservation program. The property tracks corporate-level Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions, and Scope 3 emissions if available. The property has a commitment to implement initiatives that reduce GHG emissions year over year. The property has shown an absolute reduction in emissions for at least 2 years. Emissions are either verfied by a third-party and/or published in external communications.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-conservation-program-exception
params.description: Energy conservation program exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-efficient-heating-and-cooling-systems
params.description: Energy efficient heating and cooling systems. The property doesn't use chlorofluorocarbon (CFC)-based refrigerants in heating, ventilating, and air-conditioning systems unless a third-party audit shows it's not economically feasible. The CFC-based refrigerants which are used should have a Global Warming Potential (GWP) ≤ 10. The property uses occupancy sensors on HVAC systems in back-of-house spaces, meeting rooms, and other low-traffic areas.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-efficient-heating-and-cooling-systems-exception
params.description: Energy efficient heating and cooling systems exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-efficient-lighting
params.description: Energy efficient lighting. At least 75% of the property's lighting is energy efficient, using lighting that is more than 45 lumens per watt – typically LED or CFL lightbulbs.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-efficient-lighting-exception
params.description: Energy efficient lighting exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-saving-thermostats
params.description: Energy saving thermostats. The property installed energy-saving thermostats throughout the building to conserve energy when rooms or areas are not in use. Energy-saving thermostats are devices that control heating/cooling in the building by learning temperature preferences and automatically adjusting to energy-saving temperatures as the default. The thermostats are automatically set to a temperature between 68-78 degrees F (20-26 °C), depending on seasonality. In the winter, set the thermostat to 68°F (20°C) when the room is occupied, lowering room temperature when unoccupied. In the summer, set the thermostat to 78°F (26°C) when the room is occupied.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-energy-saving-thermostats-exception
params.description: Energy saving thermostats exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-green-building-design
params.description: Output only. Green building design. True if the property has been awarded a relevant certification.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-green-building-design-exception
params.description: Output only. Green building design exception.

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-independent-organization-audits-energy-use
params.description: Independent organization audits energy use. The property conducts an energy audit at least every 5 years, the results of which are either verified by a third-party and/or published in external communications. An energy audit is a detailed assessment of the facility which provides recommendations to existing operations and procedures to improve energy efficiency, available incentives or rebates,and opportunities for improvements through renovations or upgrades. Examples of organizations that conduct credible third party audits include: Engie Impact, DNV GL (EU), Dexma, and local utility providers (they often provide energy and water audits).

params.name: sustainability-energy-efficiency-independent-organization-audits-energy-use-exception
params.description: Independent organization audits energy use exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainability-certifications-breeam-certification
params.description: BREEAM certification.

params.name: sustainability-sustainability-certifications-breeam-certification-exception
params.description: BREEAM certification exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainability-certifications-eco-certifications
params.description: The eco certificates awarded to the hotel.

params.name: sustainability-sustainability-certifications-leed-certification
params.description: LEED certification.

params.name: sustainability-sustainability-certifications-leed-certification-exception
params.description: LEED certification exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-eco-friendly-toiletries
params.description: Eco friendly toiletries. Soap, shampoo, lotion, and other toiletries provided for guests have a nationally or internationally recognized sustainability certification, such as USDA Organic, EU Organic, or cruelty-free.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-eco-friendly-toiletries-exception
params.description: Eco friendly toiletries exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-locally-sourced-food-and-beverages
params.description: Locally sourced food and beverages. Property sources locally in order to lower the environmental footprint from reduced transportation and to stimulate the local economy. Products produced less than 62 miles from the establishment are normally considered as locally produced.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-locally-sourced-food-and-beverages-exception
params.description: Locally sourced food and beverages exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-organic-cage-free-eggs
params.description: Organic cage free eggs. The property sources 100% certified organic and cage-free eggs (shell, liquid, and egg products). Cage-free means hens are able to walk, spread their wings and lay their eggs in nests).

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-organic-cage-free-eggs-exception
params.description: Organic cage free eggs exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-organic-food-and-beverages
params.description: Organic food and beverages. At least 25% of food and beverages, by spend, are certified organic. Organic means products that are certified to one of the organic standard listed in the IFOAM family of standards. Qualifying certifications include USDA Organic and EU Organic, among others.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-organic-food-and-beverages-exception
params.description: Organic food and beverages exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-responsible-purchasing-policy
params.description: Responsible purchasing policy. The property has a responsible procurement policy in place. Responsible means integration of social, ethical, and/or environmental performance factors into the procurement process when selecting suppliers.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-responsible-purchasing-policy-exception
params.description: Responsible purchasing policy exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-responsibly-sources-seafood
params.description: Responsibly sources seafood. The property does not source seafood from the Monterey Bay Aquarium Seafood Watch 'avoid' list, and must sustainably source seafood listed as 'good alternative,' 'eco-certified,' and 'best choice'. The property has a policy outlining a commitment to source Marine Stewardship Council (MSC) and/or Aquaculture Stewardship Council (ASC) Chain of Custody certified seafood.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-responsibly-sources-seafood-exception
params.description: Responsibly sources seafood exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-vegan-meals
params.description: Vegan meals. The property provides vegan menu options for guests. Vegan food does not contain animal products or byproducts.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-vegan-meals-exception
params.description: Vegan meals exception.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-vegetarian-meals
params.description: Vegetarian meals. The property provides vegetarian menu options for guests. Vegetarian food does not contain meat, poultry, fish, or seafood.

params.name: sustainability-sustainable-sourcing-vegetarian-meals-exception
params.description: Vegetarian meals exception.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-compostable-food-containers-and-cutlery
params.description: Compostable food containers and cutlery. 100% of food service containers and to-go cutlery are compostable, and reusable utensils are offered wherever possible. Compostable materials are capable of undergoing biological decomposition in a compost site, such that material is not visually distinguishable and breaks down into carbon dioxide, water, inorganic compounds, and biomass.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-compostable-food-containers-and-cutlery-exception
params.description: Compostable food containers and cutlery exception.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-composts-excess-food
params.description: Composts excess food. The property has a program and/or policy for diverting waste from landfill by composting food and yard waste, either through compost collection and off-site processing or on-site compost processing.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-composts-excess-food-exception
params.description: Composts excess food exception.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-donates-excess-food
params.description: Donates excess food. The property has a program and/or policy for diverting waste from landfill that may include efforts to donate for human consumption or divert food for animal feed.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-donates-excess-food-exception
params.description: Donates excess food exception.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-food-waste-reduction-program
params.description: Food waste reduction program. The property has established a food waste reduction and donation program, aiming to reduce food waste by half. These programs typically use tools such as the Hotel Kitchen Toolkit and others to track waste and measure progress.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-food-waste-reduction-program-exception
params.description: Food waste reduction program exception.

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-no-single-use-plastic-straws

params.name: sustainability-waste-reduction-safely-handles-hazardous-substances
params.description: Safely handles hazardous substances. The property has a hazardous waste management program aligned wit GreenSeal and LEED requirements, and meets all regulatory requirements for hazardous waste disposal and recycling. Hazardous means substances that are classified as 'hazardous' by an authoritative body (such as OSHA or DOT), are labeled with signal words such as 'Danger,' 'Caution,' 'Warning,' or are flammable, corrosive, or ignitable. Requirements include: - The property shall maintain records of the efforts it has made to replace the hazardous substances it uses with less hazardous alternatives. - An inventory of the hazardous materials stored on-site. - Products intended for cleaning, dishwashing, laundry, and pool maintenance shall be stored in clearly labeled containers. These containers shall be checked regularly for leaks, and replaced a necessary. - Spill containment devices shall be installed to collect spills, drips, or leaching of chemicals.

A water conservation audit is a detailed assessment of the facility, providing recommendations to existing operations and procedures to improve water efficiency, available incentives or rebates, and opportunities for improvements through renovations or upgrades. Examples of organizations who conduct credible third party audits include: Engie Impact, and local utility providers (they often provide energy and water audits).

params.name: context-address-address-lines
params.description: Unstructured address lines describing the lower levels of an address. Because values in `address_lines` do not have type information and may sometimes contain multiple values in a single field (for example, 'Austin, TX'), it is important that the line order is clear. The order of address lines should be 'envelope order' for the country or region of the address. In places where this can vary (for example, Japan), `address_language` is used to make it explicit (for example, 'ja' for large-to-small ordering and 'ja-Latn' or 'en' for small-to-large). In this way, the most specific line of an address can be selected based on the language. The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a `region_code` with all remaining information placed in the `address_lines`. It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved. Creating an address only containing a `region_code` and `address_lines` and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

params.name: context-address-administrative-area
params.description: Optional. Highest administrative subdivision which is used for postal addresses of a country or region. For example, this can be a state, a province, an oblast, or a prefecture. For Spain, this is the province and not the autonomous community (for example, 'Barcelona' and not 'Catalonia'). Many countries don't use an administrative area in postal addresses. For example, in Switzerland, this should be left unpopulated.

params.name: context-address-language-code
params.description: Optional. BCP-47 language code of the contents of this address (if known). This is often the UI language of the input form or is expected to match one of the languages used in the address' country/region, or their transliterated equivalents. This can affect formatting in certain countries, but is not critical to the correctness of the data and will never affect any validation or other non-formatting related operations. If this value is not known, it should be omitted (rather than specifying a possibly incorrect default). Examples: 'zh-Hant', 'ja', 'ja-Latn', 'en'.

params.name: context-address-locality
params.description: Optional. Generally refers to the city or town portion of the address. Examples: US city, IT comune, UK post town. In regions of the world where localities are not well defined or do not fit into this structure well, leave `locality` empty and use `address_lines`.

params.name: context-address-organization
params.description: Optional. The name of the organization at the address.

params.name: context-address-postal-code

params.name: context-address-address-lines
params.description: Unstructured address lines describing the lower levels of an address. Because values in `address_lines` do not have type information and may sometimes contain multiple values in a single field (for example, 'Austin, TX'), it is important that the line order is clear. The order of address lines should be 'envelope order' for the country or region of the address. In places where this can vary (for example, Japan), `address_language` is used to make it explicit (for example, 'ja' for large-to-small ordering and 'ja-Latn' or 'en' for small-to-large). In this way, the most specific line of an address can be selected based on the language. The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a `region_code` with all remaining information placed in the `address_lines`. It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved. Creating an address only containing a `region_code` and `address_lines` and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

params.name: context-address-administrative-area
params.description: Optional. Highest administrative subdivision which is used for postal addresses of a country or region. For example, this can be a state, a province, an oblast, or a prefecture. For Spain, this is the province and not the autonomous community (for example, 'Barcelona' and not 'Catalonia'). Many countries don't use an administrative area in postal addresses. For example, in Switzerland, this should be left unpopulated.

params.name: context-address-language-code
params.description: Optional. BCP-47 language code of the contents of this address (if known). This is often the UI language of the input form or is expected to match one of the languages used in the address' country/region, or their transliterated equivalents. This can affect formatting in certain countries, but is not critical to the correctness of the data and will never affect any validation or other non-formatting related operations. If this value is not known, it should be omitted (rather than specifying a possibly incorrect default). Examples: 'zh-Hant', 'ja', 'ja-Latn', 'en'.

params.name: context-address-locality
params.description: Optional. Generally refers to the city or town portion of the address. Examples: US city, IT comune, UK post town. In regions of the world where localities are not well defined or do not fit into this structure well, leave `locality` empty and use `address_lines`.

tools.name: place_action_type_metadata_list
tools.description: Returns the list of available place action types for a location or country. Optional: filter, pageToken, languageCode (plus 1 more).

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional. A filter constraining the place action types to return metadata for. The response includes entries that match the filter. We support only the following filters: 1. location=XYZ where XYZ is a string indicating the resource name of a location, in the format `locations/{location_id}`. 2. region_code=XYZ where XYZ is a Unicode CLDR region code to find available action types. If no filter is provided, all place action types are returned.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Optional. If specified, the next page of place action type metadata is retrieved. The `pageToken` is returned when a call to `placeActionTypeMetadata.list` returns more results than can fit into the requested page size.

params.name: languageCode
params.description: Optional. The IETF BCP-47 code of language to get display names in. If this language is not available, they will be provided in English.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Optional. How many action types to include per page. Default is 10, minimum is 1.

tools.name: verification_tokens_generate
tools.description: Generate a token for the provided location data to verify the location. Optional: locationId. Returns the new VerificationsGenerateInstantVerificationTokenResponse.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The location identifier associated with an unverified listing. This is the location id generated at the time that the listing was originally created. It is the final portion of a location resource name as generated by the Google My Business API. Note: the caller must be an owner or manager of this listing in order to generate a verification token. See the [location resource](/my-business/reference/rest/v4/accounts.locations) documentation for more information.
